summary: DHH announces two back-to-back programming conferences in Denmark in September 2003: LogOn Web Days in Copenhagen and JAOO 2003 in Aarhus. He plans to attend both by volunteering labor in exchange for admission. He's particularly excited about JAOO, which features several of his technical heroes including Martin Fowler, Kent Beck, the Pragmatic Programmers, and Ruby creator Matz. The post is a brief, enthusiastic look at the early-career DHH before Rails existed.
key_points: DHH was trading volunteer labor for conference admission, reflecting his pre-Rails student/early-career days
His technical idols at the time included Martin Fowler, Kent Beck, Dave Thomas, Andy Hunt, and Matz — figures who heavily influenced Rails
The conferences focused on Java, XML, and web services — the dominant enterprise technologies DHH would later rebel against
His mention of Matz and Ruby foreshadows the path that would define his career
money_quotes: I'll be trading labor for tuition on both occasions.
Most of my technical idols are represented including Martin Fowler, Kent Beck, Dave Thomas and Andy Hunt of the Pragmatic Programmers, and Yukihiro "Matz" Matsumoto — the father of Ruby.
Now I just need to figure out where to stay during the week in Aarhus, how to bring half my bookshelf for signing, and if there's any preparations against abstraction fatigue I need to make.
key_insight: A snapshot of DHH as an eager young developer volunteering at conferences to meet his idols — including Ruby's creator Matz — just before he would channel those influences into building Rails.
